About Programs Admissions Support Contact Give

Oakland, California

A new world requires a new way of learning.

Pear Tree is a social justice Montessori school where children learn to think deeply, question boldly, and build community — from preschool through 5th grade.

Schedule a Tour Support Our Mission

90%

of students exceed literacy benchmarks

80%

of families receive financial aid

95%

students of color

Education for liberation.

While only 22% of Black and Latino students nationally reach proficiency in literacy, 90% of Pear Tree students exceed benchmarks. We prove every day that when children see themselves reflected in their education, they thrive.

Our decolonial Montessori curriculum centers Black, Indigenous, and students of color — not as an afterthought, but as the foundation of everything we teach.

Our Story

Our Programs

From first steps to fifth grade, children grow through hands-on Montessori learning infused with social justice, nature, and the arts.

Preschool

Ages 2+ · Mon–Fri, 8:00 AM – 5:00 PM

Our Dragonfly and Butterfly classrooms immerse young learners in Montessori's Practical Life, Sensory, and Cultural Studies, with nature-based exploration woven into every day.

Learn more →

Elementary (TK–5)

Ages 4–11 · Mon–Fri, 8:30 AM – 3:30 PM

Across Hummingbird, Falcon, and Dolphin classrooms, students develop through project-based learning, design thinking, and a curriculum that centers their histories and communities.

Learn more →

"My child in 4th grade is doing middle school math and voraciously reading middle grade novels."

— Pear Tree Parent, via Berkeley Parents Network

What makes Pear Tree different

Peer-Reviewed

Featured in the London Review of Education for our decolonial curriculum approach. Research-backed, not just aspirational.

No Family Turned Away

80% of families receive financial aid. We believe every child deserves a liberatory education, regardless of ability to pay.

Community-Driven

From the Black Heritage Month Showcase to community potlucks, our families don't just attend — they build the school together.

Academic Excellence Through Four Pillars

PTCS supports young people in becoming producers of academic and cultural content and critical thinkers of cultural and academic consumption.

Social Justice & Civic Engagement

An historical-based curriculum engaging a deep sense of self and connection to ancestral contributions in mathematics, literature, philosophy, sciences and the arts.

Cultural Art Enrichments

Expressive arts rooted in the traditions of the students we serve — deepening connections, addressing historical trauma, and supporting agency through creation and performance.

Rigorous Academics

Montessori-aligned content to deepen independence, strengthen writing, engage with mathematics as creative expression, and navigate the sciences with wonder and awe.

Social & Emotional Learning

Intentional curriculum to deepen peer community, knowledge of self, emotional literacy, and the ability to navigate social dynamics with thoughtfulness and intention.

Multi-age classrooms
Small class sizes
Two teachers per class
Anti-bias development
Meals provided
Parent education

"You go talk to kindergartners or first-grade kids, you find a class full of science enthusiasts. Something terrible has happened between kindergarten and 12th grade."

— Carl Sagan

At Pear Tree, we protect that spark. Our children graduate curious, confident, and ready to change the world.

Join our community.

Whether you're looking for a school that sees your child, or you want to invest in education that transforms — there's a place for you here.
